## Getting Started

- **What:** formal or proof-adjacent material for ForkRaceFoldTheorems.
- **Why:** it records machine-checkable or audit-oriented evidence behind claims that should not rest on prose alone.
- **How:** follow the local validation command and keep generated artifacts tied back to their source proof or emitter.
- **Next:** read the formal ledger when you need the package-level contract.

- [VerifiedReconstruction.lean](./VerifiedReconstruction.lean)

This directory contains hand-authored theorem modules for the promoted Gnosis
Lean package, including finite readiness, reconstruction, closure, braid, the
natural 10/12/17/22 fork-race-fold-vent port map, its named vent-to-next-fork
boundary, braid packet mass certification, proof-scoped extended-aeon 43
transition plus 55 closure certification, terminal/off-cycle
`BraidPortAction.interfere` mapping for the native gnosis-uring handoff,
pressure-weighted 43 interference admission over the exact 0/18/78 leakage
trace, spherical accounting over the 120/360/1080 mass trace, 360/1080
rotational certificates, finite harmonic-quotient FFN work reduction,
finite cyclic quotient compiler reducing `[360,1080]` to `[180,180]`
representatives with a certified fivefold finite work reduction,
43 radial-exit reuse, high-latitude pressure ports, toroidal-lens perspective
certificates, twisted double-keystone pressure, packed 1560 shell roles,
self-reference knot base-return certification, closed-loop
`[10,12,17,22,43,55,10]` certification, closed-loop runtime metadata
checksum `6439`, sieve, statistical mechanics accounting, gnostic metrology,
finite indexed pressure-selector routing with
generic minimum-correctness and membership preservation lemmas, stable
equal-pressure first-candidate tie behavior, budgeted stable-tie selector
contracts, generic runtime candidate-order alignment, input-index zero candidate
preservation, over-budget tied rejection, and combined selector certificates,
pressure-budget routing monotonicity, 7/17/22 decomposition, terminal prosody
pressure, sampler-score, hard-gate, event-stage completeness, trace coherence,
decode-loop progress, closed-tail stop-maximal and open-then-stop termination
proofs, runtime-facing selection certificates, Monster resident scheduled
consume with canonical output-order preservation, FOIL loser-policy winner
invariance for drain-and-cache races, bounded integration telemetry projection,
runtime attestation transport preservation for HTTP/Flow theorem-id and summary
projections plus optional entropy certificates,
Rust runtime pressure alignment, and Glossolalia RACE identity-preservation plus
fold chargeability proofs.
